title: alphonsea stenogyna
intro: Alphonsea stenogyna, also known as the 'African white cedar', is a type of evergreen tree native to tropical Africa. Traditionally, the bark and leaves of this plant have been used for their medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Alphonsea stenogyna include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce pain and promote wound healing. Alphonsea stenogyna can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
